prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Sto pensando a te mentre ____ la cena.' (mangiare)
explanation: When you describe an action as it unfolds (eating, walking, reading), Italian reaches for the gerund. The gerund is the -ando / -endo form, and it is invariable, so there are no person or number endings to worry about. An -are verb takes -ando on its stem: mangiare gives mangi- plus -ando, so mangiando. The i of the stem stays (mangiando, not mangendo). Using -endo here (mangendo) would be borrowing the -ere / -ire ending by mistake.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ascolto la radio ____.' (lavorare)
explanation: The gerund names the activity that accompanies another (here, listening to the radio while working). An -are verb forms its gerund in -ando: lavorare gives the stem lavor- plus -ando, so lavorando. The wrong ending -endo (lavorendo) belongs to the other two conjugations, not to -are.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Si è addormentato ____ la TV.' (guardare)

translation.reveal: guardare - to watch / to look at

explanation: The gerund supplies the background action against which the main event happens (he fell asleep while watching TV). Build it from an -are verb by adding -ando to the stem: guardare gives guardando. Slipping to -endo (guardendo) crosses the conjugation line; -are keeps -ando.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Si impara ____ ogni giorno.' (studiare)
explanation: Verbs whose infinitive ends in -iare (studiare, mangiare) keep the i of the stem when forming the gerund, because the ending -ando begins with a different vowel and does not swallow it: studiare gives studi- plus -ando, so studiando. Dropping the i (studando) or switching to -endo (studendo) are the two slips to avoid.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Risparmi tempo ____ online.' (comprare)
explanation: The gerund expresses the means by which something is achieved (you save time by buying online). An -are verb builds it with -ando: comprare gives comprando. Note the trap here: comprendo would be read as a real but different word (the present 'I understand', from comprendere), so the -endo slip does not just look wrong, it lands on another verb entirely.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'È entrata in casa ____.' (cantare)
explanation: The gerund describes the manner of the main action (she came in singing). For an -are verb the ending is -ando on the bare stem: cantare gives cantando. The rival ending -endo (cantendo) is for the -ere and -ire conjugations.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ho incontrato Lia ____ il treno.' (prendere)
explanation: The gerund sets the scene for the main event (I met Lia while catching the train). The -ere conjugation forms its gerund in -endo: prendere gives prend- plus -endo, so prendendo. Borrowing -ando from the first conjugation (prendando) is the cross-class slip the -ere class invites.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ascolto musica ____ le email.' (scrivere)
explanation: The gerund names the activity going on alongside another (listening to music while writing emails). An -ere verb takes -endo: scrivere gives scrivendo. The -ando ending (scrivando) is the first-conjugation form and does not belong here.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ho imparato molto ____ questo libro.' (leggere)
explanation: The gerund expresses the means (I learnt a lot by reading this book). Leggere is an -ere verb, so its gerund is -endo on the stem: legg- plus -endo, leggendo. The double g of the stem is kept; the ending is -endo, never -ando.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Parla nel sonno ____.' (dormire)
explanation: Here is the key economy of the gerund: the -ire conjugation uses the very same ending as -ere, namely -endo. Dormire gives dorm- plus -endo, so dormendo. There is no -iendo (dormiendo) and no -ando; one ending, -endo, covers both the second and the third conjugation.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Mi ha salutato ____.' (partire)
explanation: The gerund gives the accompanying action (he greeted me as he was leaving). Partire is a plain -ire verb, and -ire forms its gerund in -endo, just like -ere: part- plus -endo, partendo. Writing -iendo (partiendo) inserts a vowel that is not there; the ending is simply -endo.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Si chiarisce tutto ____ il contesto.' (capire)
explanation: Capire belongs to the group of -ire verbs that insert -isc- in the present (io capisco), but that insertion is a present-tense affair only. The gerund is built straight from the plain stem cap-: cap- plus -endo, capendo. Carrying the -isc- across (capiscendo) is the signature miss for this group; -iendo (capiendo) is the other slip. Neither belongs: the gerund of an -ire verb is just stem plus -endo.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ti chiamo ____ di lavorare.' (finire)
explanation: Finire takes -isc- in the present (io finisco), but the gerund ignores it: the form is built on the plain stem fin- plus -endo, so finendo. The -isc- you see in finisco does not travel into the gerund (not finiscendo), and there is no extra i (not finiendo).

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ha scelto ____ la qualità al prezzo.' (preferire)
explanation: Preferire is another -isc- verb in the present (io preferisco), but the gerund is built on the bare stem prefer- plus -endo: preferendo. As with capire and finire, the present's -isc- does not appear in the gerund (not preferiscendo).

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ascolto i podcast ____ le pulizie.' (fare)
explanation: A handful of verbs build the gerund not on their short modern infinitive but on a longer underlying stem, the stem expansion that also drives their imperfect. Fare expands to fac-, exactly the stem of the imperfect facevo, and adds -endo: facendo. If you know facevo, you already know facendo. Building from the contracted infinitive (farendo) is the slip this group invites.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ha rovinato la sorpresa ____ tutto.' (dire)
explanation: Dire restores the longer stem dic- before the gerund ending, the same stem expansion you meet in the imperfect dicevo: dic- plus -endo gives dicendo. The teaching point is the parallel: dicevo and dicendo share the stem dic-. Building on the short infinitive (direndo) misses the expansion.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Si è sentito male ____ troppo caffè.' (bere)
explanation: Bere hides a longer stem bev-, the one you see in the imperfect bevevo, and the gerund is built on it: bev- plus -endo, bevendo. The same expansion, the same stem. Building on the short infinitive bere (berendo) is the miss; the gerund needs the bev- that the modern infinitive has worn away.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ha imparato l'inglese ____ canzoni.' (tradurre)
explanation: Verbs in -urre (tradurre, condurre, produrre) expand to a -uc- stem, the stem of the imperfect traducevo: traduc- plus -endo gives traducendo. The contracted infinitive tradurre conceals the c; the gerund brings it back, exactly as the imperfect does.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: 'Ha spiegato la regola ____ un esempio.' (porre)
explanation: Verbs in -orre (porre, comporre, proporre) expand to a -on- stem, the one in the imperfect ponevo: pon- plus -endo gives ponendo. The contracted -rre of the infinitive is not the stem; the gerund uses the fuller pon-, matching the imperfect.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: '____ in ritardo, ha perso il treno.' (essere)
explanation: Essere is grouped with the irregulars for company, but it is actually regular in the gerund: it builds on its own stem ess- plus -endo, giving essendo. This is also the form that opens the compound gerund (essendo andato) and the essere half of the progressive, so it is worth knowing solidly.

prompt: Forma il gerundio: '____ poco tempo, ha risposto in fretta.' (avere)
explanation: Avere, despite its irregular present (io ho), forms a perfectly regular gerund on the stem av- plus -endo: avendo. The present's ho/hai/ha shapes do not reach the gerund. Avendo is also the avere half of the compound gerund (avendo finito), so it is high-value.

prompt: Completa al presente progressivo: 'Non posso uscire, ____ un articolo.' (leggere, io)
explanation: To say an action is happening right now, Italian uses the progressive: stare conjugated for the subject plus the gerund. For io the stare form is sto, and leggere's gerund is leggendo, so sto leggendo. Two things have to be right: stare must be a finite form (sto), never the infinitive (sto leggere is the classic miss), and the second word must be the gerund, not the infinitive. The marker scores the stare form and the gerund separately.
examiner_note: One markpoint on the assembly as a whole. The engine collapses spaces, so a stare-only sub-match ('sto ') is unreliable; the full phrase is matched instead. must_not_include catches the essere-for-stare slip ('sono leggendo') and the infinitive pairing ('sto leggere'), both listed as progressive common misses. The gerund-formation skill is covered by the bare-gerund items.

prompt: Completa al presente progressivo: 'Ti sento appena, ____ ?' (dormire, tu)
explanation: The progressive is stare plus the gerund. The subject is tu, so stare is stai, and dormire's gerund is dormendo: stai dormendo. Watch the person on stare (stai, not sto) and keep the gerund pure -endo (not dormire, not dormiendo).
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint. Catches the essere slip ('sei dormendo') and the infinitive pairing ('stai dormire').

prompt: Completa al presente progressivo: 'Non disturbare il bambino, ____ adesso.' (mangiare, lui)
explanation: Stare plus the gerund builds the progressive. For lui the stare form is sta (no accent: sta, not stà), and mangiare's gerund is mangiando, so sta mangiando. The two skills are the stare person and the gerund; the marker checks each.
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint. The 3sg form 'sta' is a substring of 'stai'/'stava'/'stanno', so the full phrase is matched, not 'sta' alone.

prompt: Completa al presente progressivo: 'In questo momento ____ a un nuovo progetto.' (lavorare, noi)
explanation: 'In questo momento' (right now) calls for the progressive: stare plus the gerund. For noi the stare form is stiamo, and lavorare gives lavorando, so stiamo lavorando. A common confusion is to use the auxiliary essere (siamo lavorando), but the progressive is built with stare, not essere.
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint. 'siamo lavorando' is the essere-for-stare slip; 'stiamo lavorare' the infinitive pairing.

prompt: Completa al presente progressivo: 'Ragazzi, ____ troppo lentamente!' (scrivere, voi)
explanation: The progressive is stare plus the gerund. The subject is voi, so stare is state, and scrivere's gerund is scrivendo: state scrivendo. Keep stare finite (not the infinitive) and use stare, not essere (siete scrivendo is the auxiliary slip).
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint. Catches the essere slip ('siete scrivendo') and the infinitive pairing.

prompt: Completa al presente progressivo: 'I bambini ____ in giardino.' (giocare)
explanation: 'I bambini' (the children) is third-person plural, so stare is stanno, and giocare's gerund is giocando: stanno giocando. The subject is right there in the sentence, so map it to stanno (plural), not sta (singular). The gerund keeps the hard c of giocare (giocando).
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint. Subject (i bambini) supplied, so the cue drops the person per Rev 9. must_not_include catches the essere slip, the infinitive pairing, and the singular 'sta giocando' (wrong number).

prompt: Completa al presente progressivo: 'Zitti! La nonna ____ un discorso.' (fare)
explanation: This progressive uses an irregular gerund. Stare for la nonna (3sg) is sta, and fare's gerund is the stem-expanded facendo (fac-, as in facevo), so sta facendo. The assembly is the same as for regular verbs; only the gerund is irregular. Building fare's gerund from the short infinitive (farendo) is the miss.
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint combining the assembly with the irregular gerund facendo. Catches the contracted-infinitive gerund ('sta farendo'), the infinitive pairing ('sta fare'), and the essere slip ('è facendo'). Subject (la nonna) supplied, person dropped from cue.

prompt: Completa al passato progressivo: 'Quando hai chiamato, ____ il giornale.' (leggere, io)
explanation: The progressive works in any tense: put stare in the tense you need and keep the gerund unchanged. Here the action was ongoing in the past ('when you called, I was reading'), so stare goes into the imperfect, stavo, and the gerund stays leggendo: stavo leggendo. The gerund never changes with tense; only stare moves.
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint, past progressive. The context ('quando hai chiamato') pins past, so the present 'sto leggendo' is a wrong-tense miss (criterion 10b). Also catches the essere slip ('ero leggendo') and the infinitive pairing.

prompt: Completa al passato progressivo: 'Non ha sentito il campanello perché ____.' (dormire, lui)
explanation: An action in progress in the past takes the past progressive: stare in the imperfect plus the gerund. For lui that is stava, and dormire's gerund is dormendo, so stava dormendo ('he was sleeping'). Only stare carries the tense and person; the gerund is invariable.
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint, past progressive. Catches the essere slip ('era dormendo'), the present wrong-tense 'sta dormendo', and the infinitive pairing.

prompt: Completa al progressivo futuro: 'Domani a quest'ora ____ ancora.' (lavorare, io)
explanation: The progressive reaches into the future too: 'Domani a quest'ora' (tomorrow at this time) projects an action still in progress then. Stare goes into the future, starò, and the gerund stays lavorando: starò lavorando ('I will still be working'). As always, only stare changes tense; the accented starò is the future, distinct from the bare staro.
examiner_note: Single assembly markpoint, future progressive (B2, lighter). Catches the essere slip ('sarò lavorando'), the present wrong-tense 'sto lavorando', and the infinitive pairing. The accent on starò is tolerated-then-flagged by the engine's accent-folded fallback, so the bare 'staro' is not hard-rejected.

prompt: Forma il gerundio passato: '____ i compiti, è uscito a giocare.' (finire)
explanation: The compound gerund (gerundio passato) marks an action finished before the main one: 'having finished his homework, he went out'. It is built like the passato prossimo, avere or essere plus the past participle, but with the auxiliary in its gerund form. Finire takes avere here (it has a direct object, i compiti), so the auxiliary is avendo, and the participle is finito: avendo finito. With avere the participle does not agree with the subject.
examiner_note: Two markpoints per criterion 8: the auxiliary choice (avendo, not essendo) and the participle form. Cross-references the passato prossimo participle-form bucket (ire_ito). The auxiliary-choice skill is captured by the gerundio_passato bucket; the participle by the PP tree.

prompt: Forma il gerundio passato: 'Maria, ____ via presto, ha evitato il traffico.' (andare)
explanation: The compound gerund of a motion verb uses essendo, the gerund of essere, plus the past participle, and because the auxiliary is essere the participle agrees with the subject. The subject is Maria (feminine singular), so the participle is andata: essendo andata ('having left early'). Using avere (avendo andato) is the wrong auxiliary, and leaving the participle masculine (essendo andato) misses the agreement.
examiner_note: Two markpoints: auxiliary (essendo, not avendo) on the gerundio_passato bucket, and the feminine-singular agreement on the PP agreement bucket. Subject Maria pins the gender; the cue gives only the verb.

prompt: Forma il gerundio passato: 'I ragazzi, ____ all'alba, sono arrivati per pranzo.' (partire)

markpoints.label: partiti (participle agrees with i ragazzi, m.pl)

explanation: Partire is a motion verb, so its compound gerund is essendo plus the participle, with agreement on the participle. The subject is i ragazzi (masculine plural), so the participle is partiti: essendo partiti ('having left at dawn'). The auxiliary must be essere (essendo), not avere, and the participle must match the plural subject.
examiner_note: Masculine-plural agreement variant. Markpoint A: auxiliary; markpoint B: PP agreement (masculine plural).

prompt: Forma il gerundio passato: 'Lei, ____ già, non aveva fame.' (mangiare)
explanation: Even with a feminine subject (lei), the compound gerund of mangiare keeps the participle invariable, because mangiare takes avere and participles with avere do not agree with the subject. So the form is avendo mangiato, not 'essendo mangiata'. The trap here is to agree the participle to the feminine subject (mangiata); with avere it stays mangiato.
examiner_note: Deliberate contrast with the essere items: the subject is feminine but the avere participle stays invariable (mangiato). Markpoint B rejects the over-agreement 'mangiata' and the gerund-for-participle slip 'mangiando'.
